sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before_lines = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after_lines = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== CHANGED ROWS ===")
for i in range(64):
    if before_lines[i] != after_lines[i]:
        b = before_lines[i].replace('c', '.').replace('G', '.')
        a = after_lines[i].replace('c', '.').replace('G', '.')
        print(f"Row {i}:")
        print(f"  before: {b}")
        print(f"  after:  {a}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- After:  RRR.SS.bb.ww.bb.SS.pp.bb.gg.SS.ww.NNN
So "ww" moved from first position to last position (shift left).
Now I understand the structure:
- 3 horizontal rows with red/green controls
- Red shifts left, green shifts right
- Each row has blocks that can be cycled
The score is still 1, same as after the middle red click. So I need to figure out what the target configuration is.
Let me look at the top bar (row 1) more carefully. It shows:
NNNN.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB
The NNNN is the left green bar marker. The BBBB blocks might indicate the target positions or the number of blocks.
Wait, let me reconsider. In level 1, the goal was to make all top row blocks match bottom row blocks. In level 2, the structure is different - there are 3 horizontal rows and vertical columns.
Looking at the image, I see:
- Top row (row 17): RR.SS.bb.pp.YY.pp.NNN (with yellow markers)
- Middle row (row 26): RRR.bb.bb.ww.SS.pp.gg.SS.gg.gg.ww.NNN (after shift)
- Bottom row (row 35): RRR.SS.bb.ww.bb.SS.pp.bb.gg.SS.ww.NNN (after shift)
There are also vertical columns between these rows:
- Between top and middle: pp, bb, gg, SS columns
- Between middle and bottom: ww, ww, ww, gg columns  
- Below bottom: YY, ww, gg, bb columns
- At the very bottom: SS.SS.pp.pp.gg row
Hmm, this is complex. Let me think about what the goal might be.
Actually, looking at the top bar pattern:
Row 1: NNNNN.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB.BBBB
There are 7 BBBB groups. In level 1, there were 8 black blocks and the goal was to match 7 positions. Here there are 7 BBBB groups.
Wait, let me count more carefully. The pattern is:
NNNN G BBBB G BBBB G BBBB G BBBB G BBBB G BBBB G BBBB
That's 7 BBBB blocks. But the rows have different numbers of blocks:
- Top row: 6 blocks (SS, bb, pp, YY, pp)
- Middle row: 10 blocks
- Bottom row: 10 blocks
Hmm, that doesn't match. Let me look at the structure differently.
Actually, I think the vertical columns might also be shiftable. Let me try clicking on the green shapes to see if they shift things the other way.
But first, let me understand the current state better. The score went from 0 to 1 after clicking the middle red shape. It stayed at 1 after clicking the bottom red shape. So maybe I need to get all rows to the correct configuration.

- Bottom row: ww.SS.bb.ww.bb.SS.pp.bb.gg.SS
After shifting middle left by 1: bb.bb.ww.SS.pp.gg.SS.gg.gg.ww
After shifting bottom left by 1: SS.bb.ww.bb.SS.pp.bb.gg.SS.ww

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me extract the current state of all rows/columns more carefully
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Extract horizontal rows (main rows with red/green controls)
print("=== HORIZONTAL ROWS ===")
for row in [17, 26, 35]:
    line = lines[row].replace('c', '.').replace('G', '.')
    print(f"Row {row}: {line}")

# Extract vertical columns between rows
print("\n=== VERTICAL BLOCKS ===")
# Left column blocks (around col 18-21)
for row in [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41, 44]:
    line = lines[row].replace('c', '.').replace('G', '.')
    print(f"Row {row}: {line}")

# Right column blocks (around col 47-50)
print("\n=== RIGHT SIDE ===")
for row in [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41, 44]:
    line = lines[row].replace('c', '.').replace('G', '.')
    # Show right side
    right = line[40:]
    if right.strip():
        print(f"Row {row} right: {right}")

# Now let me try clicking green to shift right
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 26, 'col': 48}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
